docs.title: Personal Finance API
docs.text: A REST API for personal finance management built with FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and PostgreSQL.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• JWT-based authentication (register, login, logout)
  • Bearer token authorization via HTTPBearer
  • PostgreSQL with automatic Alembic migrations on startup
  • Pydantic v2 request/response validation

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install dependencies
uv sync

# Copy and fill in environment variables
cp .env.example .env

# Run the development server
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

The API will be available at http://localhost:8000. Interactive docs (Swagger UI) are at http://localhost:8000/api/docs.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Default DB_HOST PostgreSQL host — DB_PORT PostgreSQL port 5432 DB_NAME Database name — DB_USER Database user — DB_PASSWORD Database password — DB_SSLMODE SSL mode require SECRET_KEY JWT signing key — ALGORITHM JWT algorithm HS256 ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES Token TTL in minutes 1440

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
Bases: BaseSettings

Source code in app/core/config.py 
class Settings(BaseSettings):
    # Application
    app_name: str = "Personal Finance API"
    app_version: str = "0.1.0"
    debug: bool = False

    # Database
    db_host: str
    db_port: int = 5432
    db_name: str
    db_user: str
    db_password: str
    db_sslmode: str = "require"

    # JWT
    secret_key: str
    algorithm: str = "HS256"
    access_token_expire_minutes: int = 60 * 24

    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env", case_sensitive=False)

    @property
    def database_url(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"postgresql://{self.db_user}:{self.db_password}"
            f"@{self.db_host}:{self.db_port}/{self.db_name}"
            f"?sslmode={self.db_sslmode}"
        )

docs.title: get_settings() cached
get_current_user(credentials=Depends(bearer_scheme), db=Depends(get_db))
User
docs.text:
Bases: Base

Source code in app/models/user.py 
class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, index=True, nullable=False)
    username = Column(String, unique=True, index=True, nullable=False)
    hashed_password = Column(String, nullable=False)
    is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True, nullable=False)
    created_at = Column(
        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False
    )

docs.title: UserRegisterRequest
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

Source code in app/schemas/auth.py 
class UserRegisterRequest(BaseModel):
    email: EmailStr
    username: str
    password: str

    @field_validator("username")
    @classmethod
    def username_alphanumeric(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if not v.isalnum():
            raise ValueError("Username must be alphanumeric")
        return v

    @field_validator("password")
    @classmethod
    def password_min_length(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if len(v) < 8:
            raise ValueError("Password must be at least 8 characters")
        return v

docs.title: UserLoginRequest
docs.text:

class UserLoginRequest(BaseModel):

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}

docs.title: TokenResponse

Bases: BaseModel

Source code in app/schemas/auth.py 
class TokenResponse(BaseModel):
    access_token: str
    token_type: str = "bearer"

docs.title: hash_password(password)
verify_password(plain, hashed)
create_access_token(subject)
docs.text:
Source code in app/core/security.py 
def create_access_token(subject: str) -> str:
    expire = datetime.now(UTC) + timedelta(minutes=settings.access_token_expire_minutes)
    return cast(
        str,
        jwt.encode(
            {"sub": subject, "exp": expire},
            settings.secret_key,
            algorithm=settings.algorithm,
        ),
    )

docs.title: decode_access_token(token)

docs.title: InvalidCredentialsError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Source code in app/services/user_service.py 
class InvalidCredentialsError(Exception):
    pass

docs.title: create_user(db, payload)
docs.text:
Source code in app/services/user_service.py 
def create_user(db: Session, payload: UserRegisterRequest) -> User:
    if db.query(User).filter(User.email == payload.email).first():
        raise EmailAlreadyRegisteredError
    if db.query(User).filter(User.username == payload.username).first():
        raise UsernameTakenError

    user = User(
        email=payload.email,
        username=payload.username,
        hashed_password=hash_password(payload.password),
    )
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(user)
    return user

docs.title: authenticate_user(db, email, password)
docs.text:
Source code in app/services/user_service.py 
def authenticate_user(db: Session, email: str, password: str) -> User:
    user = db.query(User).filter(User.email == email).first()
    if not user or not verify_password(password, str(user.hashed_password)):
        raise InvalidCredentialsError
    return user

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: The API uses stateless JWT authentication with Bearer tokens.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
POST /api/v1/auth/register  →  create account
POST /api/v1/auth/login     →  receive access_token
GET  /api/v1/auth/me        →  use token in Authorization header
POST /api/v1/auth/logout    →  client discards token

docs.title: Register
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/register \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "user@example.com", "username": "myuser", "password": "secret123"}'

Validation rules:

  • username — alphanumeric only
  • password — minimum 8 characters

Response 201:

{
  "id": 1,
  "email": "user@example.com",
  "username": "myuser",
  "is_active": true,
  "created_at": "2026-04-21T10:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Login
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "user@example.com", "password": "secret123"}'

Response 200:

{
  "access_token": "<jwt>",
  "token_type": "bearer"
}

docs.title: Authenticated Requests
docs.text:
Pass the token in the Authorization header:

curl http://localhost:8000/api/v1/auth/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>"

docs.title: Using Swagger UI
docs.text: Navigate to http://localhost:8000/api/docs, click the Authorize button (lock icon in the top-right), paste your access_token, and click Authorize. All subsequent requests from the UI will include the Bearer header automatically.

docs.title: Token Details
docs.text:
Property Value Algorithm HS256 Default TTL 24 hours Claim sub = user ID (string)

Tokens are not revocable server-side. Logout is client-side only (discard the token).

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • uv package manager
  • PostgreSQL database (local or Neon)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/navaneethreddydevops/personal-finance-backend.git
cd personal-finance-backend

# Install all dependencies (including dev)
uv sync --dev

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the project root:

DB_HOST=your-db-host
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=your-db-name
DB_USER=your-db-user
DB_PASSWORD=your-db-password
DB_SSLMODE=require

SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key-min-32-chars

Generate a secure SECRET_KEY:

python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

docs.title: Running the Server
docs.text:
# Development (with auto-reload)
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload

# Production
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

Migrations run automatically on startup — no manual step required.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
docker build -t personal-finance-backend .
docker run -p 8000:8000 --env-file .env personal-finance-backend

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
# Lint and auto-fix
uv run ruff check --fix app/
uv run ruff format app/

# Type check
uv run mypy app

# Pre-commit (runs on every git commit automatically)
uv run pre-commit run --all-files

docs.title: Adding a Migration
docs.text:
uv run alembic revision --autogenerate -m "describe your change"
uv run alembic upgrade head
